# N+1 Query Fix — Code Rewrite Instructions

**Generated by:** Diagnostician Pipeline v1.0.0  
**Timestamp:** 2026-05-24T12:27:24+05:30  
**Root Cause Category:** `APPLICATION_LAYER_N_PLUS_1`

---

## ⚠️ IMPORTANT: This Is Not a Database Problem

> The query optimizer, indexes, and schema are **functioning perfectly**. EXPLAIN confirms all lookups use Primary Key Index Scans at ~1ms each. **No migration, no new index, and no schema change is required.**

The 1,250ms latency is caused entirely by **500 sequential network round-trips** from this application-layer anti-pattern:

```typescript
// ❌ BROKEN — 501 queries: 1 for posts + 500 individual author fetches
const posts = await prisma.post.findMany({ where: { status: 'PUBLISHED' } });
for (const post of posts) {
  post.authorData = await prisma.author.findUnique({ where: { id: post.authorId } });
}
```

| Metric | Before | After (Fix H1) |
|---|:---:|:---:|
| Total DB Queries | **501** | **1** |
| Network Round-Trips | **501** | **1** |
| Total Latency | **~1,250ms** | **~50ms** |
| Latency Reduction | — | **~96%** |
| DB Migration Required | — | **None** |
| Schema Change Required | — | **None** |

---

## Fix H1 — Prisma `include` Eager Loading (Recommended)

Replace the entire loop with a single `findMany` that uses Prisma's built-in relation eager loading. Prisma compiles this into a single SQL `LEFT JOIN`.

```typescript
// ✅ FIXED — 1 query total: single JOIN fetching all posts + authors
const posts = await prisma.post.findMany({
  where: { status: 'PUBLISHED' },
  include: {
    author: true,   // Prisma resolves the relation in one JOIN
  },
});

// Access author data directly — no secondary fetching needed
for (const post of posts) {
  console.log(post.author.name, post.author.email);
}
```

**Generated SQL equivalent:**
```sql
SELECT
  p.id, p.title, p.status, p.author_id,
  a.id, a.name, a.email
FROM posts p
LEFT JOIN authors a ON p.author_id = a.id
WHERE p.status = 'PUBLISHED';
```

---

## Fix H2 — Prisma `include` with `select` (Production-Grade)

For production APIs, restrict which fields are fetched to minimise payload size and serialization cost:

```typescript
// ✅ FIXED — Selective fields, single JOIN, no over-fetching
const posts = await prisma.post.findMany({
  where: { status: 'PUBLISHED' },
  select: {
    id:     true,
    title:  true,
    author: {
      select: {
        id:    true,
        name:  true,
        email: true,
      },
    },
  },
});
```

**Why this is preferred over H1 in production:**
- Does not fetch unused columns (e.g., `updatedAt`, internal metadata).
- Reduces JSON serialization cost proportionally to field count.
- Provides compile-time type safety on the returned shape via Prisma's generated types.

---

## Fix H3 — DataLoader Batch Pattern (For GraphQL / Dynamic Trees)

Use this approach when `include` is impractical (e.g., deeply nested GraphQL resolvers, dynamic field selection):

```typescript
// Step 1: Fetch all posts (1 query)
const posts = await prisma.post.findMany({ where: { status: 'PUBLISHED' } });

// Step 2: Extract and deduplicate unique author IDs
const uniqueAuthorIds = [...new Set(posts.map(p => p.authorId))];

// Step 3: Batch fetch all required authors in a single IN-clause query (1 query)
const authors = await prisma.author.findMany({
  where: { id: { in: uniqueAuthorIds } },
});

// Step 4: Build an O(1) lookup map
const authorMap = new Map(authors.map(a => [a.id, a]));

// Step 5: Stitch authors back onto posts in memory — zero extra DB queries
const enrichedPosts = posts.map(post => ({
  ...post,
  author: authorMap.get(post.authorId),
}));
```

**Why this handles edge cases H1/H2 don't:**
- If 100 posts share 1 author → only 1 author lookup is made (deduplication via `Set`).
- Works inside GraphQL resolvers where Prisma `include` doesn't compose cleanly.
- Total queries: always exactly **2**, regardless of how many posts or authors exist.

---

## Decision Matrix: Which Fix to Use

| Scenario | Recommended Fix |
|---|---|
| Standard REST API route — fetching posts with authors | **H1** — `include: { author: true }` |
| Production API with strict payload size requirements | **H2** — `include` + `select` |
| GraphQL resolver / DataLoader context | **H3** — Batch + Map pattern |
| Deeply nested recursive trees (e.g., comments + authors + replies) | **H3** with a DataLoader library |

---

## Verification

After applying the fix, run the intercepted route again and confirm in your logs:

```
✅ Expected: prisma:query — 1 query logged (single JOIN)
❌ Was:      prisma:query — 501 queries logged (loop pattern)
```

Enable Prisma query logging temporarily to verify:
```typescript
const prisma = new PrismaClient({ log: ['query'] });
```

You should see **exactly one** `prisma:query` log line for the fixed route, containing a `LEFT JOIN`.
